title: Classification of Bayon Faces
highlights: Digital 3D models of historic buildings or cultural heritage objects are useful for preservation. Not only can we store them permanently, but the models can supply a clear guideline for the restoration process. 3D models also provide sufficient information about geometrical characteristics that may help archaeologists to inspect and classify the objects. Currently, we are working on a 3D digital-archiving project of the Bayon Temple. It is a building of stonework that was built in the 12th century in Cambodia. It is famous for its towers with four faces at the four cardinal  ...    into three groups based on subjective criteria. In this chapter, we explore a more objective way to classify the faces by using measured 3D geometrical models. After alignment of 3D faces in the same coordinate system, orientation, and  ...
